message:
fix(security): reject leading-zero IPv4 octets to prevent SSRF octal bypass

Add check in parse_ipv4() that rejects octets with leading zeros (e.g.
0177.0.0.1 which some parsers interpret as octal 127.0.0.1). This closes
an SSRF filter bypass where attackers could use octal-encoded loopback
addresses to reach internal services.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
